summary:
Variations in carotid endarterectomy use are primarily driven by population needs related to symptoms like stroke or transient ischemic attack, which correlate with broader vascular disease burdens. These clinical indications lead to proportional increases in procedure usage based on regional disease prevalence, reflecting real-world patient demand rather than anatomical variability.
